top_findings.summary: The skill requests "Bash(cmd:*)" which allows execution of any bash command without restriction, while its stated primary purpose is to manage cron job scheduler operations (e.g., generating configurations, providing guidance). Cron job management typically requires only specific commands (e.g., `crontab`, `grep`) and file operations (read/write/edit) on relevant configuration files. Unrestricted bash command execution is excessive and could enable actions unrelated to cron scheduling, such as accessing sensitive system data or performing unauthorized modifications, posing a security risk.
